behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: environment seam (real git subprocess rename detection) plus module/interface seam (status parsing to category counters). REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE apply: if the tool forgets rename flags (e.g., rename detection disabled), a mock returning idealized rename records would miss the real failure. Integration was considered, but rejected because behavior text requires classifying real repository changes, which depends on actual git subprocess output format and flags. Agentic/Workflow rejected: no AI/ML judgment or multi-agent pipeline in this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI against that repo, and assert report shows 1 rename, 0 added for that rename, and 0 deleted for that rename.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The test must execute real git + real repo state; lower-level tests can miss subprocess/flag failures.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: environment seam (real git diff output for binary content) crossing into parser/counting modules. REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE apply: binary diff behavior and output shape come from git; mocked text can hide parser crashes seen only with real subprocess output. Integration was considered, but rejected because behavior explicitly depends on handling binary diffs produced by the real git command path. Agentic/Workflow rejected: deterministic tooling, no model output dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp git repo, add/modify a binary file (e.g., random bytes), run CLI, assert process exits successfully and binary file is included in the correct count/category.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Crash-safety here is tied to real subprocess output and repo content, requiring System.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: environment seam (git subprocess behavior on unborn HEAD/no commits). REAL-INFRA applies: this failure is about real command exit/error behavior from git in that repo state. Integration was considered, but rejected because the key condition is subprocess semantics in an actual repository with no commit history. Agentic/Workflow rejected: no AI/ML dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a real git repo with no commits, run CLI, assert zero exit error handling and an empty structured report shape instead of failure output.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Behavior hinges on real git error/exit semantics, which lower levels can only approximate.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: environment seam (real repository history via git commands) plus module aggregation seam (mapping statuses to totals). REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE apply: mocked histories can accidentally mirror implementation assumptions; real git history traversal catches wrong ranges/commands/count logic. Integration was considered, but rejected because behavior text ties correctness to actual repository history, requiring real git execution over real commits. Agentic/Workflow rejected: no non-deterministic model steps.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real temp repo with a known sequence of commits containing add/delete/rename/modify events, run CLI for the target window, and assert each reported count equals the known ground truth.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Validating against actual history requires real repo + git subprocess, so System is the minimum.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: environment seam (real git index vs working-tree semantics) crossing CLI diff-selection logic. REAL-INFRA strongly applies: staged/unstaged parity depends on actual index/worktree state transitions and git subprocess behavior. Integration was considered, but rejected because in-process fakes cannot faithfully reproduce git index semantics and may hide mismatches. Agentic/Workflow rejected: deterministic CLI/domain with no model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, create equivalent content changes once as staged-only and once as unstaged-only, run CLI in both states, and assert structured stdout reports are identical.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: This behavior directly targets real git index/worktree behavior, requiring System-level execution.
